title: Adding FFT and L-BFGS to Glassbox
summary:
I spent the last two days working on Glassbox. I wanted the model to find internal constants like omega in sin(omega*x) automatically. I added a function using numpy FFT to look at the data and guess the frequency. 

I also put in a nested L-BFGS optimizer. It runs 5 steps on every candidate before checking the fitness. This helps get better values for omega and phi. I had to change the initialization range for omega from [0.5, 2.0] to [0.3, 6.0] so it finds more patterns. Now the code actually shows the omega value in the formula, like sin(3.2*x). 

I also added early stopping so the search does not run forever if it is not improving. The performance on test data is much better now. I am running it on 600 samples across [-15, 15] to make sure the FFT stays accurate.
commitMessages: feat(evolution): Add FFT frequency detection and nested BFGS for internal constants
